channel.title: Blog.jetbrains.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Blog.jetbrains.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Expanding Our Core Web Development Support in PyCharm 2026.1
item.description: With PyCharm 2026.1, our core IDE experience continues to evolve as we’re bringing a broader set of professional-grade web tools to all users for free. Everyone, from beginners to backend-first developers, is getting access to a substantial set of JavaScript, TypeScript, and CSS features that were previously only available with a Pro subscription. React, JavaScript, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/03/expanding-our-core-web-development-support-in-pycharm-2026-1/

item.title: WebStorm 2026.1: Service-powered TypeScript Engine, Junie, Claude Agent, and Codex in the AI chat, Framework Updates, and More
item.description: WebStorm 2026.1 is now available! This release focuses on the everyday web development workflows where IDE support matters most, helping you stay productive in large TypeScript projects, making it easy to keep up with frameworks that evolve quickly, and bringing AI tools into the IDE so you don’t have to switch contexts. The highlights of […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/webstorm/2026/03/webstorm-2026-1/

item.title: IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 Is Out!
item.description: IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 is here, and it comes packed with an array of new features and enhancements to elevate your coding experience! You can download this latest release from our website or update to it directly from inside the IDE, via the free Toolbox App, or using snap packages for Ubuntu. As always, all new features are brought […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/intellij-idea-2026-1/

item.title: What’s fixed in IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1
item.description: Welcome to the overview of fixes and improvements in IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1. In this release, we have resolved over 1,000 bugs and usability issues, including 334 reported by users. Below are the most impactful changes that will help you work with greater confidence every day. Performance We continue to prioritize reliability, working to improve application […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/whats-fixed-intellij-idea-2026-1/

item.title: Introducing JetBrains Central: An Open System for Agentic Software Development
item.description: AI is beginning to change how software is produced. Instead of just assisting developers inside the editor, AI agents now investigate issues, generate code, run tests, and execute multi-step workflows. As this work scales, software development extends beyond individual tools or sessions. It becomes a distributed system of agents, environments, and workflows that operate across […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/blog/2026/03/24/introducing-jetbrains-central-an-open-system-for-agentic-software-development/

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Astral: What It Means for PyCharm Users
item.description: On March 19, OpenAI announced that it would acquire Astral, the company behind uv, Ruff, and ty. The Astral team, led by founder Charlie Marsh, will join OpenAI’s Codex team. The deal is subject to regulatory approval. First and foremost: congratulations to Charlie Marsh and the entire Astral team. They shipped some of the most […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/03/openai-acquires-astral-what-it-means-for-pycharm-users/

item.title: KotlinConf’26 Speakers: In Conversation with Josh Long
item.description: “There’s never been a better time to be a JVM or Spring developer.” The Spring ecosystem has evolved dramatically over the past decade, from traditional enterprise applications to microservices, distributed systems, and now AI-powered services. Few people have witnessed that evolution as closely as Josh Long, who has served as Spring’s first Developer Advocate since […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/kotlin/2026/03/kotlinconf-26-speakers-in-conversation-with-josh-long/

item.title: The New Role of Data Teams in the Agentic Analytics Era
item.description: Last week, in the first part of this series, we explained why two analysts can produce two different answers from the same data, and why that problem gets worse with AI agents. Without a shared semantic layer defining metrics and business logic, AI will generate answers faster, but not more reliably. Today we’ll focus on […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/databao/2026/03/new-role-of-data-teams/

item.title: Rider 2026.1 Release Candidate Is Out!
item.description: The Rider 2026.1 Release Candidate is ready for you to try. This upcoming release brings improved support for the .NET ecosystem and game development workflows, as well as refinements to the overall developer experience. Rider 2026.1 allows you to work with file-based C# programs and offers an improved MAUI development experience on Windows, mixed-mode debugging, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/dotnet/2026/03/20/rider-2026-1-release-candidate/

item.title: ReSharper 2026.1 Release Candidate Released!
item.description: The ReSharper 2026.1 Release Candidate is ready for you to try. This release focuses on making everyday .NET development faster and more predictable, with improvements to code analysis and language support, a new way to monitor runtime performance, and continued work on stability and responsiveness in Visual Studio. If you’re ready to explore what’s coming, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/dotnet/2026/03/20/resharper-2026-1-release-candidate/

item.title: KotlinConf 2026: Talks to Help You Navigate the Schedule
item.description: The full KotlinConf’26 schedule is finally live, and it’s packed! With parallel tracks, deep-dive sessions, and back-to-back talks, planning your time can feel overwhelming. When almost every session looks interesting, deciding where to spend your time isn’t easy. To help you navigate it all, the Kotlin team has selected a few talks worth adding to […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/kotlin/2026/03/kotlinconf-2026-talks-schedule/

item.title: Why Your AI Governance Is Holding You Back, and You Don’t Even Know It
item.description: Most enterprises claim to govern their AI use. They have policy documents, review boards, approval flows, and sandbox environments. On paper, control exists. Then agents enter real software delivery workflows. They generate code, refactor systems, open pull requests, query internal data, trigger automations, and coordinate across tools. They move from experiment to execution. At that […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2026/03/why-your-ai-governance-is-holding-you-back-and-you-don-t-even-know-it/

item.title: Core JavaScript and TypeScript Features Become Free in IntelliJ IDEA
item.description: Modern Java development often involves web technologies. To make this workflow more accessible and smoother, we’re making some core JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, and CSS features – previously included with the Ultimate subscription only – available for free in IntelliJ IDEA v2026.1. JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, CSS, and React Support Enjoy a comprehensive set of features for […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/js-ts-free-support/

item.title: IntelliJ IDEA’s New Kotlin Coroutine Inspections, Explained
item.description: This article was written by an external contributor. Every technology has its misuses, and different ecosystems use different approaches to prevent them. In the Kotlin ecosystem, I believe the philosophy has always been to make APIs so good that correct usage is simple and intuitive, while misuse is harder and more complicated. This differs from […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/intellij-idea-s-new-kotlin-coroutine-inspections-explained/

item.title: $ teamcity From the Command Line
item.description: Start builds, tail logs, and manage agents and queues – without switching windows. Whether you are debugging a failed build, managing build agents, or scripting your deployment pipeline (or your AI coding agent is doing so) – there are times when opening a browser is one step too many. TeamCity CLI is a lightweight, open-source […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/teamcity/2026/03/teamcity-cli/

item.title: Google Summer of Code 2026 Is Here: Contribute to Kotlin
item.description: The Kotlin Foundation is joining Google Summer of Code (GSoC) 2026! If you are a student or an eligible contributor looking to spend your summer working on a real-world open-source project, this is your chance to make a meaningful impact on the Kotlin ecosystem while also benefiting from the mentorship of experienced engineers. Take part […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/kotlin/2026/03/gsoc-2026-contribute-to-kotlin/

item.title: CloudBees vs TeamCity: Enterprise CI/CD Beyond Jenkins
item.description: Many organizations adopt Jenkins because it’s flexible and widely supported. Over time, however, that flexibility often turns into operational overhead: maintaining plugins, debugging pipelines, and coordinating upgrades across teams. CloudBees CI and JetBrains TeamCity represent two different ways of addressing this problem. CloudBees CI builds on Jenkins, adding enterprise-grade governance, centralized management, and commercial support. […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/teamcity/2026/03/cloudbees-vs-teamcity/
